content: Hi, I'm Frankie. I'm a PhD student in Computational Linguistics, and I'm based in Amsterdam, Netherlands. I love running and trying to stay active, especially when I'm traveling for work.
Nice to meet you, Frankie! Computational Linguistics is a fascinating field. What inspired you to pursue it?

I've always been interested in language and how it works, and I think it's really cool to be able to use computational methods to analyze and understand language. I'm also really into Japanese cuisine and trying new foods when I travel.
That's great! Japanese cuisine is amazing. Have you had a chance to try any new foods recently?
Actually, yes! I was just in Tokyo for a conference and I tried some amazing ramen at a little restaurant in Shibuya. I'm also a big fan of vintage cars and I've been thinking of buying one soon.
Tokyo is a great city! Shibuya is such a fun area to explore. What kind of vintage car are you thinking of buying?

I'm thinking of buying a 1969 Ford Mustang. I've always loved the design and I think it would be a great project to work on. I'm also really into mechanical keyboards and I've been building my own custom keyboards in my free time.
A 1969 Ford Mustang is a classic! Mechanical keyboards are also really interesting. What got you into building your own keyboards?

I've always been interested in tech and how things work, and I think it's really cool to be able to customize my own keyboard to fit my needs. I've been building keyboards for about 2 years now, and I've learned a lot about electronics and design.
That's amazing! Building your own keyboards is such a unique hobby. Do you have any plans for your PhD research after you finish your degree?

Yeah, I'm thinking of pursuing a career in industry, maybe working for a company that specializes in natural language processing. I've been talking to my advisor, Dr. Lee, about my options and she's been really helpful.
That sounds like a great plan! Natural language processing is a really exciting field. Do you have any other hobbies or interests that you're passionate about?
Yeah, I'm also really into marathon running and I've been training for a few months now. I'm planning to run the Amsterdam Marathon in October, which will be my first marathon ever.
That's amazing! Running a marathon is such an accomplishment. I'm sure you'll do great. Have you had any particularly challenging or rewarding experiences while training for the marathon?
